title: pressure cooker mushroom risotto
ingredients: 1.5 lbs mushrooms (a mixture is fine), cleaned, trimmed, and thinly sliced
1/2 cup white wine or sake (or just a little water or stock to deglaze)
1 oz grated or shredded high quality parmesan
(optional) more parmesan and any finely minced herb for serving
instructions: Heat olive oil and butter in the pressure cooker and add all of the mushrooms. Cook until the moisture is released and reduced slightly. It doesn't need to be fully dry and browned, but that’s fine too.
Add onion and garlic and cook until barely softened. Add rice and cook until starting to become translucent around the edges, about 5 minutes.
Stir soy sauce and miso paste until evenly distributed. Add wine and cook until the alcohol has cooked off, about 2–3 minutes.
Pour the stock into the pot (strain if needed). Seal and cook on low pressure for 5 minutes (about 10 psi). Release the steam valve and open the pressure cooker.
Stir in parmesan and a few cracks of fresh black pepper. Check seasoning - adding salt as needed. Adjust consistency if needed.
Serve with extra grated parmesan and herbs at the table.
notes: Pairs well with honey butter roast carrots. Optional: For deeper mushroom flavor, add about 1 oz dried porcini or shiitake -- heat the stock before cooking and steep the mushrooms in it (5 minutes for porcini, up to 1 hour for shiitake), then chop and add to the fresh mushrooms. Strain the soaking liquid/stock combo before using, leaving behind any grit. If the risotto turns out too wet, consider using slightly less broth, especially when adding wine or sake. If too dry, loosen with a bit of stock or cream.
